message: Stabilize symbol map hover details
Focus symbol dependency routes
Add on-demand symbol dependency RPC
Show selected symbol edges independent of zoom
Preserve selected symbol focus
Prune symbol map edges around focus
Prototype rectangular symbol map
Stack symbol modules by dependency rank
Lay out symbol modules as weighted network

Add atlas layout kernel with capacity-constrained power diagrams

New self-contained module (src/atlas, Preact + SVG) independent of the
existing UI. Phase 0 of the network-extended city-map visualization:

- Power diagram via radical-axis half-plane clipping, recording the
  neighbor site per cell edge (adjacency comes free for Newton steps
  and shared-edge rendering)
- Capacity iteration: damped Lloyd relaxation, then diagonal-Newton
  weight adaptation against the post-move diagram; empty cells jump
  straight to the re-entry weight bound instead of crawling back
- Warm-start via applyGraphChanges for weight/node/clip changes,
  the basis for animated updates
- Clip regions: rect, circle (polygonized, 16-gon = circleSegments 16)
  and arbitrary convex polygons for nested file→symbol layouts
- Force-directed seeding pipeline, GeoJSON boundary output,
  language-agnostic AtlasGraph contract with pluggable weight scoring
- Playground (pnpm dev:atlas): synthetic graphs or a fixture of this
  repository with real per-file symbols, error heat coloring,
  convergence sparkline, warm-start mutation buttons

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add concentric module rings with nested capacity layouts

Phase 1 of the atlas view: modules placed on rings by topological rank,
each subdivided by the capacity kernel.

- contracts/modules: file→module derivation (src/<x> heuristic, custom
  ModuleIdOf hook for monorepo package detection later) with aggregated
  cross-module edge weights
- kernel/topoRank: Tarjan SCC + longest-path rank, cycle-tolerant
- kernel/ringLayout: rank = ring, area-proportional circles, chord-based
  non-overlap guarantee, barycenter angular ordering, invertible direction
- playground/ringsController: one capacity layout per module circle,
  warm-started as a whole via applyRingsChanges on any graph change
- RingsMapSvg: cursor-centric wheel zoom, drag pan, coarse LOD that hides
  symbol nets when zoomed out
- Synthetic generator now builds a module DAG with several modules per
  level (a plain layer chain degenerates into one module per ring)

Fixes found along the way:
- option merge treated explicit undefined as a value, clobbering solver
  defaults into NaN weights (cells collapse to empty); merge now skips
  undefined and a regression test pins it
- rAF stops in hidden tabs, freezing the solver; fall back to a timer
  with a larger step batch so layouts keep converging in background

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add symbol LOD, dependency focus, and a test overlay layer

Phase 2 of the atlas view, plus interaction performance fixes.

Semantic zoom & traversal:
- Zooming past 2.2x switches file edges to symbol-level nodes and
  reference edges; file cells stay subdivided at every zoom
- Symbol references from snapshot symbolImports (file→symbol; the LSP
  provider will upgrade these to symbol→symbol later) and a synthetic
  generator for playground graphs
- Selecting a symbol lists referenced-by / references with viewport
  jumps — call-hierarchy style traversal
- Exported symbols get an emerald cell tint and label priority: public
  API names appear at a lower zoom than private symbols

Dependency-path extraction (module / file / symbol):
- kernel/reach: bidirectional reachable subgraph with only the edges on
  paths through the root, split into downstream/upstream sets
- Focus mode dims everything off-path; edges colored by direction
  (orange = depends on, cyan = depended on), matching the panel arrows

Test layer:
- contracts/layers: pluggable test/source classifier and test→subject
  matching (name match first, then most-imported source)
- Capacity layout subdivides source files only; tests render as
  translucent dashed circles stacked on their covered source cell,
  toggleable from the controls

Performance & rendering correctness:
- Zoom/pan writes the viewBox straight to the DOM; LOD re-renders are
  throttled to 120ms (a wheel storm re-rendered ~1.4k SVG nodes per
  event and froze the tab)
- Viewport culling drops off-screen cells/labels/edges from the DOM
- Labels clamp to screen-px sizes and hide when the viewport sits
  inside the cell; vector-effect non-scaling-stroke applied per shape
  (it does not inherit from <g>, so strokes were scaling with zoom)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Keep tests inside the layout with a muted fill and layer toggles

Reverts the overlay approach: test files are regular capacity-layout
members again, so cell areas read directly as the test:implementation
ratio per module. Test cells render with a muted slate fill and label;
the covers-target jump stays in the details panel.

The controls now list layers discovered from the graph (source, test)
with per-layer visibility. Hiding the test layer re-flows the map via
the warm-start path so tests melt out and back in; hiding source leaves
module circles as scaffolding.

Also fixes a Preact gotcha: style {display: undefined} is a no-op
assignment, so a group once hidden never came back — use "" to clear.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Mark public API by site dots and add dockable collapsible panel

Exported symbols: the cell area carries no signal (the internal graph
does), so drop the emerald cell fill. The site dot is the marker now —
green, slightly larger, and surfacing from file-level zoom (>0.8x)
together with its label, while private symbols wait for symbol mode.
Public API nodes are clickable for reference traversal that early too.

Panel: DevTools-style docking (auto | right | bottom, auto follows the
window aspect ratio on resize) and the controls / stats / selection
sections are collapsible. Native <details> with onToggle-synced state,
since Preact would otherwise reapply the open attribute on re-render
and undo the user's fold.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Make panel sections scrollable and float debug stats over the map

Bottom dock clipped the controls section with no way to reach the rest;
sections now scroll within the dock height. The stats section is debug
info, so it floats at the bottom-right of the map, folded by default.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Load the playwright monorepo and budget solver work by time

sprawlens itself is single-package, so add microsoft/playwright as a
real monorepo data source: 1404 files / 4349 symbols across 39 modules
(packages/* and tests/*), stripped into a 1.6MB JSON fetched lazily
from public-atlas/. Module derivation now treats container dirs
(src, packages, apps, libs, tests) as two-segment module ids, and
test-target matching builds its import counts in one pass over edges.

Fixed-count solver stepping blocked the main thread for seconds at
this scale; the tick loop now runs on a time budget (10ms visible /
150ms hidden) and the nested-symbol sync spreads work across frames
with a rotating cursor.

Ambient edges default to off — the aggregated module dependencies stay
available behind the edges toggle; selection and focus edges are
unaffected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add LSP call-hierarchy provider and on-demand symbol edges

Phase 3: real symbol→symbol reference edges via the Language Server
Protocol, replacing the static file→symbol approximation on demand.

- src/atlas/server: dependency-free JSON-RPC framing, a minimal LSP
  client that spawns typescript-language-server --stdio (documentSymbol
  → prepareCallHierarchy → incoming/outgoingCalls), and an HTTP server
  (pnpm dev:atlas-server) hosting one language server per registered
  repo (name=path args; sprawlens and playwright wired)
- Playground fetches the call hierarchy lazily when a symbol is
  selected (vite proxies /api), resolves the returned refs to known
  atlas ids by line containment → name → file, and merges them into
  the edge set, so the panel lists and direction-colored edges show
  real callers/callees
- Integration test runs against the real language server on this repo

Known limit: files outside a tsconfig project (src/atlas is excluded
from the root tsconfig here) resolve callers poorly; standard repos
like playwright are unaffected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Throttle rendering on monorepo-scale graphs

playwright-sized maps (4k+ symbol cells) crashed during interaction:
every symbol cell and ~1.5k exported dots lived in the DOM from low
zoom. Maps above 2000 symbol cells now raise the inner-detail threshold
into viewport-culling territory (zoom > 1.6) and skip the early
public-API dot display, so the overview carries file cells only.
Measured: overview DOM 7.1k → 1.4k polygons, 100 wheel events in 157ms.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Budget nested-symbol rendering by visible element count

Fixed zoom thresholds still overloaded mid-zoom views on monorepo
graphs (hundreds of file cells, each with nets, dots and labels).
Nested-symbol detail is now decided per frame from what is actually in
the viewport: visible file cells claim their symbol cells in descending
screen-area order until a ~1500-element budget is spent. Big cells in
view always show their internals, dense views stay flat, and the
selection's file is always included.

Measured on playwright: mid-zoom 335 polygons / 232 dots (was: every
visible cell's internals), 100 wheel events in 81ms.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Skip symbols below a minimum on-screen size

A symbol cell smaller than ~12px on screen is unreadable and
unclickable, so rendering it is pure overhead. Such cells are skipped
entirely (net, dot, label) and no longer consume the visibility budget,
which now goes to symbols that are actually discernible.

playwright overview drops to file cells only (1405 polygons, ~0 symbol
elements); mid-zoom 234 polygons / 131 dots.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Cull by the view rect at every zoom level

Viewport culling was gated behind zoom > 1.5, so panning at low zoom
kept off-screen polygons in the DOM. The gate is gone: visibility is
decided by the committed view rect alone. Panning the map fully
off-screen at 1.3x now leaves zero cell polygons rendered.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Defer LOD commits until the zoom gesture settles

Committing the view every 120ms during a gesture re-ran LOD and
inserted hundreds of nodes mid-zoom, which made zooming back out from
deep zoom janky. The commit is now a trailing debounce: interaction
stays on the cheap scaled-viewBox path and the DOM updates once when
the gesture pauses. Visibility filtering also runs once per render
instead of per layer (cells were tested three times each).

Measured on playwright: 40-event zoom-out gesture in 37ms.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Eliminate GC storms during zoom and add a lightbringer perf scenario

The reported crashes at playwright density were not rendering volume
but garbage collection: a lightbringer trace drilldown over a
zoom-in/out scenario showed ~3s of major-GC marking inside a 2.4s
zoom cycle, with 350ms mark-compact pauses landing on the next
interaction.

Allocation sources removed:
- per-render rebuilds of labels / exportedIds / testFileIds (regex over
  every node) / testTargets / the flattened inner-cell array — now refs
  refreshed only when the graph or a nested layout actually changes
- per-tick locOf Map and alive-set churn in the nested-symbol sync
- per-symbol string parsing for parent-file lookup (hottest self-time
  frame) — replaced by the symbol-meta map
- per-commit site Maps and file-cell arrays in the SVG, now memoized on
  the rings/innerCells identity

Dev-mode vnode hooks (preact/debug + prefresh) were ~40% of remaining
self time and feed the same GC: devtools are disabled for the atlas
entry and ATLAS_HMR=0 drops prefresh for heavy-map sessions.

e2e/atlas-zoom.spec.ts (lightbringer) measures the scenario per step;
ATLAS_BASE_URL points it at a prod preview. Results: prod build and
no-HMR dev show zero long tasks across every zoom step; default dev
drops from 350ms pauses to occasional ~270ms (prefresh-driven).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Document the atlas dev modes and perf scenario

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
